2604.05051.title: This Treatment Works, Right? Evaluating LLM Sensitivity to Patient Question Framing in Medical QA
2604.05051.paper_summary: Patients are increasingly turning to large language models (LLMs) with medical questions that are complex and difficult to articulate clearly. However, LLMs are sensitive to prompt phrasings and can be influenced by the way questions are worded. Ideally, LLMs should respond consistently regardless of phrasing, particularly when grounded in the same underlying evidence. We investigate this through a systematic evaluation in a controlled retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) setting for medical question answering (QA), where expert-selected documents are used rather than retrieved automatically. We examine two dimensions of patient query variation: question framing (positive vs. negative) and language style (technical vs. plain language). We construct a dataset of 6,614 query pairs grounded in clinical trial abstracts and evaluate response consistency across eight LLMs. Our findings show that positively- and negatively-framed pairs are significantly more likely to produce contradictory conclusions than same-framing pairs. This framing effect is further amplified in multi-turn conversations, where sustained persuasion increases inconsistency. We find no significant interaction between framing and language style. Our results demonstrate that LLM responses in medical QA can be systematically influenced through query phrasing alone, even when grounded in the same evidence, highlighting the importance of phrasing robustness as an evaluation criterion for RAG-based systems in high-stakes settings.
2604.05051.paper_summary_zh: 患者越來越多地尋求大型語言模型 (LLMs) 來解答複雜且難以清晰表達的醫療問題。<lb/>然而，LLMs 對提示的措辭非常敏感，並且可能受到問題表述方式的影響。<lb/>理想情況下，LLMs 應該無論措辭如何都能一致回應，特別是在基於相同的基本證據時。<lb/>我們通過在一個受控的檢索增強生成 (RAG) 環境中對醫療問題回答 (QA) 進行系統評估來研究這一點，該環境中使用的是專家選擇的文檔，而不是自動檢索的。<lb/>我們考察患者查詢變異的兩個維度：問題框架（正面與負面）和語言風格（技術性與通俗語言）。<lb/>我們構建了一個包含 6,614 對查詢的數據集，這些查詢基於臨床試驗摘要，並評估八個 LLM 的回應一致性。<lb/>我們的發現顯示，正面和負面框架的對比組比同框架組更可能產生矛盾的結論。<lb/>這種框架效應在多輪對話中進一步加強，持續的說服會增加不一致性。<lb/>我們沒有發現框架與語言風格之間有顯著的相互作用。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，在醫療 QA 中，LLM 的回應可以僅通過查詢措辭系統性地受到影響，即使基於相同的證據，這突顯了措辭穩健性作為高風險環境中 RAG 基於系統的評估標準的重要性。
2604.05051.author: Hye Sun Yun et.al.
2604.05051.authors: Hye Sun Yun, Geetika Kapoor, Michael Mackert, Ramez Kouzy, Wei Xu, Junyi Jessy Li, Byron C. Wallace
